name: Chhole Alu
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Potatoes, boiled, peeled and cubed
2 large Onions, cut into thin rings
A few Mint leaves, finely sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak channa in water overnight.
2. Next morning, boil the soaked channa with a pinch of soda and salt until tender and dry.
3. Heat 4 tablespoons ghee in a pan. Add grated ginger and chopped onions; fry until the mixture turns limp and golden brown.
4. Add diced tomatoes and spices (coriander, chili powder, cardamom) to the pan; cook until the ghee starts separating from the vegetables.
5. Mash the cooked channa with a wooden spoon into a smooth paste and add 1 cup water and powdered jaggery. Mix well and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and gently stir in the slit green chillies, cubed potatoes, and tamarind pulp (soaked in 4 tablespoons water until soft).
7. Continue cooking until the gravy thickens.
8. Turn off the heat. Garnish with onion rings, minced chili peppers, coriander leaves, mint leaves, and lime wedges before serving.
